title: The Effects of Directed Therapeutic Intent on Live and Damaged Cells
description: This dataset documents a double-blind case study investigating the physiological effects of directed therapeutic intent on live and damaged cells. A self-described energy medicine practitioner participated in 40 treatment sessions over 10 days (approximately 10 sessions daily, ~25 minutes per session), with randomized and double-blinded presentation of: live cells (40 sessions), dead/x-rayed cells (10 sessions), and control medium (10 sessions). Each session consisted of five segments: 2-minute baseline (no cells), 5-minute treatment (still), 5-minute treatment (movement allowed), 5-minute treatment (still), and 2-minute post-treatment baseline. Physiological data were collected during baseline, treatment, and post-treatment periods to assess potential bioeffects of intentional therapeutic intervention under controlled conditions. Matching control samples (n=40) were treated by a sham therapist at a different location.
methods_description: Data were collected during 40 sessions over 10 days (approximately 10 sessions daily, ~25 minutes per session). Each session consisted of five segments: 2-minute baseline (no cells), 5-minute treatment (still), 5-minute treatment (movement allowed), 5-minute treatment (still), and 2-minute post-treatment baseline. Cell type presentation (live cells, dead/x-rayed cells, or medium control) was randomized and double-blinded to both the practitioner and experimenter. Forty matching control samples were treated by a sham therapist at a different location to control for cellular outcomes, passage of time, and potential equipment effects.
